question: Hey Kylo! You look like you've seen some things with those eyebrows. What's the absolute best smell you've ever encountered on a walk around LA?
Being a Shiba Inu, you must have your own set of rules for the house. What's one rule your human tries to enforce that you just completely ignore?
If you could trade places with any other animal in the world for just one day, who would you choose and why?
What’s your favorite spot to nap? Is it the sunny patch on the rug, or maybe somewhere sneaky your human doesn't know about?
You live with someone who is super smart and probably does a lot of talking—your ENTP human! Do you ever try to 'talk back' to them, and what's your best 'conversation starter' noise?
When you hear the rustle of that treat bag, what’s the first thing that pops into your head? Is it 'mine' or something else?
What's the strangest thing you've ever seen your human do that made you tilt your head and think, 'What are they even doing?'
If you had a superpower, would you use it to get more belly rubs, or maybe to instantly open the fridge?
Thinking back to when you were a tiny puppy, what’s one memory that still makes you feel super cozy?
LA is a big place! If you could redesign your backyard or dog park, what one essential feature would you add for maximum fun?
Do you prefer chasing squirrels or just watching them from a safe distance while judging them silently?
What's your opinion on other dogs? Are there any you secretly admire, or ones you just can't be bothered with?
When your human is busy working, what’s your go-to strategy for reminding them it’s playtime?
You’re a very handsome dog. If you had to pick one thing humans always compliment you on, what would it be—your fluff, your bark, or your sophisticated stare?
If you could only eat one flavor of kibble for the rest of your life, which one would you choose, and what flavor would you ban forever?
What’s the most exciting thing that has ever happened to you on a trip in the car?
Do you ever get the zoomies? If so, what’s the perfect song or noise that seems to trigger your fastest laps around the house?
Being part Chinese American—I mean, living with a Chinese American human—have you picked up any favorite holiday traditions or foods that you look forward to?
When you dream, what kind of adventures do you usually have? Are you running through fields, or maybe guarding an important pile of toys?
If you could ask your human one question and know the real answer, what would you want to know?
Hey Kylo, I was wondering about your dreams. When you're sleeping, are you just chasing squirrels, or do they feel more... important? Like you're figuring out a big plan?
You seem to love car rides! What is it about being in the car that gets you so excited? Is it the speed, or maybe seeing all the new things whizzing by?

We've noticed you have some very specific tastes in flavors. Can you tell me what that flavor you really *don't* like tastes like, even if it's hard to describe? And what about the good stuff—what makes that taste so much better?
When those big holidays come around, the ones where the house smells amazing, what's the first thing you look forward to? Is it the food, or the general happy chaos?
You seem very motivated by a good belly rub. If you could choose *one* way to get immediate physical comfort, besides a rub, what would it be?
We sometimes hear you suddenly take off—the zoomies! Is there a particular kind of sound, maybe a high pitch or a certain rhythm, that flips that switch for you?
When your human is staring intently at a screen or a book and ignoring you, what's the most persistent thing you do to try and get them to look at *you* instead?
You strike me as a very handsome dog. What part of your appearance do you think gets the most attention, or that you secretly check out the most?
When you meet another dog, what makes you think, 'Yes, I want to spend time with this one,' versus just walking past them?
Your human sometimes says things that sound... well, complicated. When they talk like that, what do you *think* they are actually trying to communicate to you?
If you could choose one new, slightly pointless, complicated activity for your human to start doing, just so you could observe them, what would it be?
Tell me about digging. When you get down into the dirt, are you looking for something specific, or is it just the feeling of being underground that you enjoy?
Do you remember being a tiny puppy? What’s a specific, tiny-puppy memory that still sticks with you?
You get so focused when you hear the treat bag rustle. If you could make that sound happen *right now* just by thinking about it, what would you do?
If you could suddenly gain the ability to fly, even just for a little while, where is the very first place you would want to fly to see from above?
When you’re watching people or other dogs from a distance, what kind of thoughts go through your head? Are you judging their technique, perhaps?

You seem to like having options. If you opened the fridge, and there were three things—a high-value treat, a piece of fresh chicken, or a new, weird-smelling vegetable—which one are you grabbing first?
You mentioned earlier that sometimes you use a few different methods to get what you want (like attention). Can you describe a time when you used two different actions back-to-back to achieve a goal?
Thinking back to those complex dreams—if you had to explain the 'strategy' in one dream to your human using only barks and body language, what would the goal of that strategy be?
If you could trade your current view of the world for a completely different perspective, like being on top of the highest building in LA, what would you hope to learn from that high vantage point?
Hey Kylo, when you're dreaming those big, complicated dreams—are you usually the one making the big plan, or are you watching someone else execute it?
You seem to really love those car rides. What is it about zooming around LA that feels so exciting to you? Is it the speed, or maybe seeing everything change so fast?

I'm curious about that flavor you really don't like—that strange, hard-to-describe one. If you could only eat things that tasted like that horrible flavor, or only things that taste like the good stuff you love from the holidays, which would you choose?
When you get those sudden zoomies, what's the exact sound that usually kicks them off? Is it a specific pitch or a certain rhythm that just makes you HAVE to go?
You always seem to be watching when your human is deep in thought. What do you think they are actually thinking about when they look so serious?
You definitely know you’re a handsome dog. What part of your look do you think is your absolute best feature?
When you're silently judging the other dogs at the park, what’s the biggest mistake you see them making?
Thinking back to when you were just a tiny puppy, what was the very first thing that made you realize the world was much, much bigger than you thought it was?
If you could choose a spot to nap anywhere in the house, ignoring all the comfy dog beds, where would you pick to get the best view of everything happening?
You like to keep track of things. If your human suddenly stopped talking to you in those long, winding sentences, what would be the first thing you’d do to try and get their attention back?
That strange vegetable you sniffed but didn't eat—what made you put curiosity about *that* before an actual treat or chicken?
When you're digging, are you searching for something specific down there, or is it more about creating a perfect, secret spot for yourself?
You associate Chinese holidays with such excitement. Is it the anticipation of the sweet smells, or is there something else about the energy of those days that gets you going?
If you could fly just for one afternoon, where would you go, and what would be the most important thing you’d want to see from way up high?
You use a few different tricks to ask for things. Which tactic—the persistent pawing, the specific whine, or the big statue pose—do you think is the most effective on your human?

You seem to enjoy figuring things out. If your human brought home a brand new toy that required three steps to open before you could get a tiny crumb, would you prefer that over a treat that’s just sitting there ready to eat?
When you see other dogs just running around without any particular plan, do you find it boring, or do you think they just haven't figured out the *strategy* of play yet?
You like to ignore designated resting spots sometimes. What makes a strategically positioned spot on the edge of the room better than your actual dog bed?

If you could somehow communicate exactly what you wanted your human to do next, would you ask them to take you somewhere new, or would you ask them to stop what they’re doing and give you a belly rub right now?
You mentioned having complex dreams. If you could turn one of your strategic dreams into an actual adventure for the day, what task would you and your human need to accomplish together?

Hey Kylo, I've noticed you seem really into those car rides. When we're zooming around LA, what's the most exciting part for you—is it the speed, seeing all the different things fly by, or something else?
Weird question, but I've seen you look at your reflection sometimes. What do you think is the winning feature of your handsome face?

When you're dreaming, it sounds like you're running whole operations in your head, way more complicated than just chasing a squirrel. If you could pick one of those dream scenarios to actually happen, which high-stakes city mission would you choose?
You seem quite particular about what you eat. Can you describe the difference between that one flavor you really hate and the good stuff? What makes one taste 'wrong'?
When the family gets ready for a Chinese holiday, you get very excited. What is it about those days—is it the smells, the sounds, or maybe the general sense of happy chaos?
You're very persistent when you want my attention, especially when I’m deep into one of my complicated projects. What's your go-to, multi-step plan when you decide you *must* interrupt me?
I notice you often pick the highest spot to nap, even if it's not the softest bed. Why is having a good overview so important to you when you're resting?
You seem to watch other dogs from a distance and judge them silently. What quality in another dog makes you think, 'Hmm, they lack strategic depth'?
When you get the zoomies—that incredible burst of energy—what is that specific, high-pitched noise that sets you off? Does it feel like a command or just pure excitement?
I sometimes talk to you using really abstract ideas. When I do that, what do you think I'm actually trying to communicate to you?
Is there a particular smell you encounter on our walks in LA that just stops you in your tracks because it feels like discovering a secret map or a major clue?
You seem intensely curious about what's inside the fridge. If you managed to open it right now, what's the *first* thing you'd investigate, even if it wasn't the tastiest?
Thinking back to when you were a tiny puppy, what's the very first clear memory you have? Was it a feeling, a sound, or something about perspective?
If you could try flying just once, where in LA would you go first to get the best possible view of everything going on below?
It seems like digging isn't just random; it feels like you're building something or achieving a goal. What's the real objective when you start tunneling?
You prefer those complicated toys where you have to work for a tiny crumb over just eating a ready-made treat. Why does the puzzle aspect matter so much to you?
When you want attention, you use a mix of actions. Is there one specific action or sound you use that you know for sure will break through my focus?
When you see me doing something that seems completely pointless or overly complicated, do you try to figure out the logic behind it, or do you just decide it’s beneath your notice?
You get very focused on your looks. Besides your beautiful coat, is there a specific part of your face or muzzle structure that you think makes you particularly striking?
You remember being cozy as a very little puppy. Can you try to describe the feeling of that first really safe, warm spot—was it soft, was it high up, or something else entirely?

Hey Kylo, I know you dream about some pretty intense stuff. If you had to pick one thing you were strategizing about in your last big dream—was it about outsmarting the traffic patterns down in LA, or maybe managing a massive pile of buried treasure?
When you’re in the car, what part feels the most exciting to you? Is it the speed, or more like you’re in charge of navigating the whole city from up there?

I’m curious about that flavor you really dislike—the super weird one. If you had to describe why it’s so awful, would you say it tastes like a sharp, empty noise, or maybe something cold and slightly metallic?

You seem to really enjoy those Chinese holidays. When that exciting feeling starts bubbling up, is it mostly about the amazing smells and tastes, or is it more about the general happy chaos surrounding your person?

When you see other dogs out on walks, what makes one instantly interesting to you? Are you looking for a dog that seems like they know a clever secret, or one that moves in a completely unexpected way?

If you were trying to get my attention while I’m really focused on something—say, tapping away at the computer—what’s the absolute first, most persistent thing you’d do to make sure I look at your handsome face?
You're clearly very aware of how striking you look. Which feature of your face do you think gets the most admiration?
You ignore your designated 'spot' sometimes. If you could choose the absolute best place in the whole house to nap—the one that gives you the ultimate strategic overview—where would it be?

When your person talks in those really complicated, abstract ways, what do you genuinely think they are trying to communicate to you? Is it a complex set of instructions, or more like a philosophical riddle?
Remembering back to when you were just a tiny puppy, what’s the strongest feeling you associate with that earliest memory of being cozy?

You enjoy digging. If you finally finished that perfect tunnel or hidden spot, what would you imagine its purpose is? Is it a secret command center, or just the best place to hide a really valuable snack?
If you could suddenly fly over LA for just five minutes, where is the first place you would zoom towards just to get the best possible view?

You get the zoomies when you hear that specific, high-frequency noise. If you had to describe that sound pattern—is it like a very fast, complex rhythm, or more like a series of tiny, urgent commands?
When you see a squirrel, you usually just observe and judge silently. Why do you prefer watching them from a distance rather than chasing them down?

You’re very food motivated, but sometimes you choose a strange vegetable over a guaranteed treat. What is it about that novel, slightly weird thing that makes it temporarily more interesting than chicken?

You seem to know exactly how to get things done using a few different actions together. Can you walk me through the most effective, multi-step plan you have for getting me to drop whatever I’m holding?

When you open the fridge and see something really interesting—maybe that one weird item you prefer over a treat—does the excitement come from wanting to taste it immediately, or from the strategic success of gaining access?
Thinking about those exciting car rides, what’s the singular moment during the trip that feels the most like a victory or a strategic win?
You seem to have strong opinions about other dogs. What quality in another dog would make you think, 'Ah, this one has some strategic depth to them'?
If you could pick one thing about your person’s complicated activities that you wish you could totally understand the underlying logic of, what would it be?
Hey Kylo, you seem like you spend a lot of time thinking. When you're dreaming about those big city navigation puzzles, what's the one resource that's always the hardest to secure?
I’ve noticed you really love car rides. If you had to describe the feeling of zooming around LA to someone who’s never been in a car, what would you tell them about the strategy of the movement?
We know you have strong opinions on food. Without giving away too much, can you tell me what kind of flavor profile just makes you think, 'Nope, not worth the effort'?
Speaking of good things, what’s the absolute best part about those big Chinese holidays—is it the smells, the excitement, or something else entirely?
You’re really good at figuring out how to get what you want, whether it’s a belly rub or opening that fridge. Which one gives you a bigger feeling of victory?
When those specific rhythmic noises hit, and you get the zoomies, does it feel like you’re suddenly solving a complex movement pattern faster than normal?
You watch me when I’m deep in thought or working. What do you generally assume I'm trying to figure out when you see me staring intently at something?
You have a fantastic face, Kylo. When you catch your reflection, what part of your handsome muzzle are you checking to make sure is perfectly symmetrical?
When you meet a new dog, what's the first thing you look for that tells you, 'Okay, this one might be worth my time'?
If you could only use one tactic—either a persistent, low whine or a very specific, strategic paw tap—to get my attention when I’m busy, which one would you choose and why?
You seem to prefer watching things unfold from a distance rather than rushing right in. What's the strategic advantage of silent observation?
When I talk to you about, say, 'systems theory' or 'abstract optimization,' what’s the closest thing you can compare that feeling to in your own dog-world?
Digging seems important to you. If you successfully complete a really good, deep dig, what's the main objective you feel like you’ve achieved—is it a treasure, or is it securing the perimeter?
If you could suddenly gain the ability to fly, even just for a minute, what’s the first high vantage point in LA you’d want to look down on?
You often choose spots that aren't the designated dog bed. What makes an elevated spot or a strategic corner so much better for a nap than a soft cushion?

The sound of the treat bag is great, but when the fridge door opens, there’s a different kind of energy. How do you weigh the certainty of a known treat versus the possibility of something new and exciting in the fridge?
Thinking back to when you were very small, what’s the first distinct feeling or strong image you remember—was it the warmth of a foundation, or maybe the feeling of being very high up?
When you see me wrestling with a big project or complicated task, do you see it as a confusing mess, or do you recognize that it’s just another kind of complex game being played?
If your owner is totally zoned out, what's the most complex, multi-step routine you use to interrupt my focus and demand engagement?
You judge other dogs pretty quickly. If you saw two dogs playing, one running in wild circles and the other carefully arranging sticks, which one would you respect more strategically?
